Facts.text: Battle of Nineveh: Byzantine Emperor Heraclius defeats Sassanid forces during the Byzantine-Sassanid War
1st Crusaders capture & plunder Mara, Syria
Discovery of a sarcophagus supposedly containing the body of Mary Magdalene in the crypt of the church of Saint-Maximin, southeastern France
Order of the Dragon is first created by Emperor Sigismund, then King of Hungary, and his wife, Queen Barbara of Celje, following the battle for possession of Bosnia
Jews are expelled from Schlettstadt Alsace by Emperor Frederick III
Composer Adrian Willaert moves from Milan to Venice
English "Barebones" Parliament ends
Brandenburgs army occupies Stettin
Russian and Prussian troops occupy Stralsund [NS=12/23]
Pope Clement XIV proclaims a universal jubilee
Bank of North America opens at Carpenter's Hall, Philadelphia
Mexico officially recognized as an independent nation by US
1st Canadian coins circulated (1 cent, 5 cent, 10 cent & 20 cent)
Battle of Dumfries, Virginia
Naval Engagement at Yazoo River, MS (USS CAIRO torpedoed)
Joseph Rainey (South Carolina) becomes 1st African American to serve in US House of Representatives
Jules Janssen discovers dark lines in solar corona spectrum
1st Test match played at the Adelaide Oval
Anti-Jewish violence breaks out in Bucharest, Romania
Belo Horizonte, the first planned city of Brazil, founded
Rudolph Dirks' 1st "The Katzenjammer Kids" cartoon strip appears in NY Journal
1st first-class cricket game between Australian states NSW and Tasmania
1st case of plague on Oahu, Hawaii
George F Bryant of Boston patents the wooden golf tee
Roger Casement completes report about abuses in Belgian Congo
CMS McClellan's "Leah Kleschna" premieres in NYC
Oscar Straus, 1st Jewish US government member, appointed Secretary of Commerce
Delhi replaces Calcutta as the capital of India.
Hebrew language officially used to teach in Palestinian schools
The largest one-day percentage drop in the history of Dow Jones Industrial Average, down 24.39%
1st all-metal aircraft (Junkers J-1) test flown at Dessau, Germany
Russian troops overrun Hamadan, Persia
French troop train derails in French Alps killing 543
Rev Edward Flanagan forms Boys Town outside Omaha, Nebraska
Spanish troops leave Morocco
Arthur Heinman coins term "motel" from motor hotel; opens Motel Inn, San Luis Obispo
Robert Nichols and Maurice Browne's play "Wings over Europe" premieres in New York City
Baseball Rules Committee greatly revises the rule book; a ball bouncing into the stands is not a home run, now a double
Start of the 1st Australia v West Indies Test (at Adelaide)
Japanese government of Imukai forms
S N Behrman's "Biography" premieres in NYC
USSR & China resume diplomatic relations
1937 NFL Draft: Sam Francis from University of Nebraska first pick by Philadelphia Eagles
1938 NFL Draft: Corbett Davis from University of Indiana first pick by Cleveland Rams
Japanese aircraft shell & sink US gunboat Panay on Yangtze River in China. (Japan apologized & eventually paid US $2.2M in reparations)
NBC & RCA sends 1st mobile-TV vans onto the streets of NY
Russ Indigirka capsizes in blizzard off Japanese coast; 750 die
British troops conquer Sidi el-Barrani
European reservists on Java mobilize
German occupying army do a house search in Paris looking for Jews
Soviet 20th army recaptures Soljetsjnogorsk
A fire in a hostel in St. John's, Newfoundland, kills 100 people
German offensive in South Western Stalingrad
Special Court of justice convicts Dutch Nazi leader Mussert to death
'Tide' laundry detergent introduced
Abandoned ice plant in Manahttan, New York City, collapses during a fire, shearing a tenement building next door, killing 38
United Mine Workers union withdrew from AFL
Malayan Emergency: Batang Kali Massacre - 14 members of the Scots Guards stationed in Malaysia allegedly massacre 24 unarmed civilians and set fire to the village.
AL votes 7-1 rejecting legalizing the spitball
16th Heisman Trophy Award: Vic Janowicz, Ohio State (HB)
Baseball owners vote to drop 4-year old bonus & high school rule
1st prototype of hovercraft patented by British engineer Christoper Cockerell
Commencement of the Irish Republican Army's Border Campaign.
Maj Adrian Drew flies 1,943 kph in F-101 Voodoo
US announces manufacture of Borazon (harder than diamond)

Willem J. Kolff and his team at Ohio's Cleveland Clinic removed the heart from a dog and replaced it with a pneumatic pump which kept the dog alive for 90 minutes, proving the viability of the artificial heart
Dutch Social Democratic Party ministers, including Prime Minister Willem Drees dismissed
Indian leg spin bowler Subhash "Fergie" Gupte takes 9-102 v W Indies at Kanpur
Jack Brabham finishes 4th in season-ending US Grand Prix at Sebring to become first Australian F1 World Drivers Championship winner; takes title by 4 points from Briton Tony Brooks
UN Committee on Peaceful Use of Outer Space is established
Amateur radio satellite Oscar 1 launched with military Discoverer 36
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Frank Sinatra, Jr returned by kidnappersafter his father paid the $240,000 ransom demanded
Kenya (formerly British East Africa) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Cleveland Browns' Frank Ryan sets club record of 5 TD passes
Doug Walters makes maiden Test ton in 1st Test, goes on to 155
The Beatles' last concert in Great Britain at Capitol Theatre in Cardiff, Wales
Vivian Beaumont Theater opens at 65th St & Amsterdam Ave NYC
US Supreme Courts votes 4-3 allowing Braves to move to Atlanta
US launches Pioneer 8 into solar orbit
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Decathlon Bill Toomey achieves world record-score of 8417 points
Offices of Banca Nazionale dell'Agricoltura in Piazza Fontana, Milan, are bombed, killing 14
Polish government proclaims price rise
Small Astronomy Satellite Explorer 42 launched to study X-rays
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Canada begins selling Olympic coins ($5 & $10 silver coins)
San Diego files anti-trust against NL (stopping Padres move to DC)
Gas stove explodes & starts fire killing 138 in Mecca, Saudi Arabia
Spaniard Manuel Orantes beats Wojtek Fibak of Poland 5–7, 6–2, 0–6, 7–6, 6–1 to win his first and only season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix tennis title in Houston, Texas
Gold hits record $462.50 an ounce
President of Pakistan, Zia-ul-Haq, confers Nishan-e-Imtiaz on Nobel laureate Dr Abdus Salam

Apple makes its initial public offering on the US stock market - 38 years later it would become the 1st US company valued at over $1 trillion, 1st over $2 trillion two years after that, and 1st over $3 trillion three years after that
US copyright law amended to include computer programs
Gambia & Senegal sign agreement to be known as Senegambia in Feb 1982
$9,800,000 in cash stolen from money transport car in NYC

The "Snowplow Game": when a snowstorm holds a New England vs Miami game scoreless, Mark Henderson, a convict on work release, on Patriots coach Ron Meyer order, clears the path for John Smith's attempt, which wins the game for the Patriots, 3-0
A truck bomb explodes at US Embassy in Kuwait
248 US soldiers & 8 crew members die in Arrow Air charter crash
DC-8 crashes near Gander, Newfoundland; 258 die
NASA launches space vehicle S-207
James "Bone Crusher" Smith TKO's WBA champ Tim Witherspoon in Madison Square Garden
Russian Tupolev-134 crashes in East Berlin, 70 killed
South African journalist and activist Zwelakhe Sisulu arrested at John Vorster Square
Mookie Blaylock sets NBA record of 13 steals in a game
Okla's sets NCAA record of 33 steals vs Centenary
Rollermania at Madison Square Garden, Eastern Express beats Midwest Pioneers
3 trains collide in London, 40 die
NYC Subway system adds new stations (Z line)
PLO leader Yasi Arafat accepts Israel's right to exist
US accuses Iraq of dragging its feet on dates for talks
US ambassador to Kuwait, Nathaniel Howell, leaves Kuwait
Abuja replaces Lagos as the capital city of Nigeria
NJ Nets set NBA record of 22 blocks beating Nuggets 121-81
Orion Pictures filed Chapter 11 for bankruptcy protection
Tampa Bay Buccaneer Dexter Manley retires after failing drug test
58th Heisman Trophy Award: Gino Torretta, U of Miami (QB)
6.8-7.5 earthquake strikes Flores Island (tsunami kills 3,000)
NY Giants lose 19-0 to Phoenix Cardinals
WAQX 104.3 (Q-104) rock format replaces WNCN classic format in NYC
Amendment to make it illegal to physically desecrate the flag turned down by senate 63-36 (need 2/3 vote)
CBC announces Radio Canada International service to end on March 31
NBA referees return to work after striking
Assassination attempt on Uday Hussein, heir to Iraqi leader Saddam Hussain
Carlos the Jackal, "professional revolutionary", goes on trial in Paris
Florida releases Alex Arias, the last original Marlin
Japanese train builders (Maglev) claim world speed record at 332 mph
SWAT team shoots John E Armstrong in Florida, freeing 2 young hostages
TWA 800 hearings end
On This Day launches MusicOrb.com and TodayinSport.com, separate sites for music and sports history, now our music and sport channels
Peugeot produces its last car at the Ryton Plant, signaling the end of mass car production in Coventry, formerly a major center of the British motor industry

Andrei Klepach, Deputy Economic Minister of Russia, states that Russia has entered a recession, with two quarters of contraction expected, meaning Russia will fall short of reaching the 6.8% growth forecast for 2008
Mosconi Cup nine-ball pool, London, England: Europe regains title with an 11-8 win over US; MVP: Darren Appleton (England)
77th Heisman Trophy Award: Robert Griffin III, Baylor (QB)
The United States announces sanctions on the two dozen companies who assisted Iran with their nuclear program
Over 200 pro-democracy activists arrested in Hong Kong
UN climate change talks in Peru continue past scheduled time as negotiations continue to stall
COP21 climate change summit in Paris reaches a deal between 195 countries to limit the rise in the global average temperature to less than 2°C above pre-industrial levels
First women ever elected in Saudi Arabia in municipal council elections
Paolo Gentiloni is sworn in as Prime Minister of Italy, succeeding Matteo Renzi
Australian shopping mall firm Westfield is bought by French property company Unibail-Rodamco for $15.7 billion
Democrat Doug Jones defeats Republican Roy Moore for Alabama Senate seat in upset win marked by allegations of sexual misconduct against Moore
China built 88 out of world's 143 skyscrapers in 2018 more than any country ever (buildings over 200 meters/656 ft)
Michael Cohen, former lawyer for US PresidentDonald Trumpsentenced to three years in prison for campaign-finance violations and tax fraud
Russian cosmonauts Oleg Kononenko and Sergey Prokopyev compete complex spacewalk to fix hole in Soyuz spacecraft
Seven mass graves in former Islamic State area discovered containing hundreds of bodies, some tortured, near Albu Kamal, eastern Syria
Deepest point on land on earth identified under Denman Glacier, east Antarctica at 3.5km (11,500ft) below sea level
Three Scottish brothers, Ewan (27), Jamie (26), and Lachlan (21) MacLean, depart in a rowboat from La Gomera in the Canary Islands to cross the Atlantic Ocean, headed for the Caribbean Islands
British PMBoris Johnsonannounces a emergency COVID-19 booster program to protect the NHS and stem a possible incoming “tidal wave of Omicron”

'Calculated and targeted execution' leaves three people shot and killed, including two police officers, with the three killers also killed in a six hour gun fight near Wieambilla, west of Brisbane, Australia
Emma Tucker appointed editor-in-chief of The Wall Street Journal, first woman to lead the paper
The city of Richmond, Virginia, capital of the Confederacy during the US Civil War, removes its last Confederate statue, of Ambrose P. Hill
U.N. General Assembly votes overwhelmingly to demand a humanitarian cease-fire in Gaza, amid reports 18,000 Palestinians have been killed, 70% children and women, with over 80% now homeless
A suspected Chinese spy, with links to Prince Andrew, is banned from the UK by a government tribunal
PopeClement VIIapproves Organization of Jewish Community of Rome

The Royal Society censuresEdmond Halleyfor suggesting in a paper titled 'Some considerations about the cause of the universal deluge' that the story of Noah's flood could be an account of a cometary impact
In Vienna,Ludwig van Beethoven(22) receives his first lesson in music composition from FranzJoseph Haydn(60)
Hawaiian King David Kalakaua is 1st king to visit the US as guest ofUlysses S. Grantat 1st US state dinner at the White House
Joseph Pulitzerbegins publishing the "St. Louis Post-Dispatch"
James Weldon Johnson's poem "Lift Every Voice and Sing", is set to music by his brother J. Rosamond Johnson; the hymn becomes known as the Black national anthem
German historianTheodor Mommsenis awarded the Nobel prize for Literature for his historical writing and especially his monumental work, "A History of Rome"
Aristide Briandforms French war government
Maurice Ravel's ballet "La Valse" premieres in Paris
Last Qajar Shah of Iran is deposed;Reza Shah Pahlavitakes over
Medina surrenders to Saudi forces led by Sultan AbdulazizIbn Saud
Eddie Shorehits Toronto Maple Leaf star Ace Bailey from behind, fracturing his skull and ending his career
Chinese leaderChiang Kai-shekdeclares war on Japan
United Nations accepts six Manhattan blocks as a gift fromJohn D. Rockefeller Jr.
Chuck Yeagerreaches Mach 2.43 in Bell X-1A rocket plane
Film adaptation of "The Rose Tattoo", starringAnna MagnaniandBurt Lancaster, premieres at the Astor Theatre in Times Square, NYC
Ballon d'Or: Juventus' Argentine forward Omar Sívori wins award for best football player in Europe ahead of Spanish midfielderLuis Suárezand English forward Johnny Haynes
Argentina asks for extradition of ex-PresidentJuan Perón
Kenya becomes an independent republic withJomo Kenyattaas its first President
Chicago halfbackGale Sayersties NFL record for most touchdowns in a game with 6 in 61–20 victory over San Francisco 49ers at Wrigley Field
"A Man for All Seasons" based on the play by Robert Bolt, directed by Fred Zinnemann and starringPaul Scofieldpremieres in New York (Best Picture 1967)
Arthur Ashebecomes 1st black person be ranked #1 in tennis
Northern Ireland Prime MinisterTerence O'Neillreceives overwhelming support from Unionist Members of Parliament (MPs) at Stormont
Rolling Stones film TV show "Rock and Roll Circus"- guest performers include The Who, Jethro Tull, Taj Mahal, Marianne Faithfull, andJohn LennonandYoko Ono; the program never airs aired
Comedy film "The Magic Christian", starringPeter SellersandRingo Starrpremieres in London, England
Romanian tennis starIlie Năstasefinishes ATP Masters Grand Prix with a perfect 6-0 record to win the rich season ending event at the Coubertin Stadium, Paris
"The Poseidon Adventure", starringGene Hackman,Ernest Borgnine,Shelley Winters, and Jack Albertson premieres in NYC
Sara Jane Moore pleads guilty to trying to kill US PresidentGerald Ford(serves 32 years)
QuarterbackJoe Namathplays his last game with the NY Jets
"Rainman" directed byBarry Levinsonand starringDustin HoffmanandTom Cruisepremieres in New York (Best Picture 1989)
Sandra Miller of Queens suesMike Tysonfor sexual harassment
Japanese crown princeNaruhitoannounces engagement to Masaka Owada
Israeli Prime MinisterShimon Peresaddresses both houses of the US Congress
Fed judge sentences Autumn Jackson, who claims to beBill Cosby's daughter, to 26 months for trying to extort $40 million from him
Red Sox sign Pedro Martinez to record 6 year $69 million contract
US Supreme Court releases its 5-4 decision in Bush v. Gore, settling the recount dispute in Florida's 2000 presidential election inGeorge W. Bush's favor, thus handing him the presidency overAl Gore
Winona Ryderis arrested on shoplifting charges in Beverly Hills, California
Ballon d'Or: Inter/Real Madrid forwardRonaldois named best football player in Europe for a second time ahead of Real Madrid left backRoberto Carlosand Bayern Munich goalkeeper Oliver Kahn
Paul Martinbecomes Prime Minister of Canada after resignation ofJean Chretien
Ballon d'Or: Real Madrid forwardCristiano Ronaldoclaims award for the 4th time; record winning margin of 429 fromLionel Messi
Bill English is sworn in as New Zealand Prime Minister, succeedingJohn Key
Philippines PresidentRodrigo Duterteboasts he killed susected criminals personally when mayor of Davao City
American Media Inc. publisher of "National Enquirer" admits paying hush money to mistresses of US PresidentDonald Trump
British Prime MinisterTheresa Maysurvives 200-117 no-confidence vote from her own Conservative Party
British General Election is won byBoris Johnson's Conservative Party in a landslide victory with an 80-seat majority, while the Scottish National Party wins 48 of 59 seats in Scotland
Chinese leaderXi Jinpingsays the country will reduce its carbon intensity by 65% by 2030
Media personalityPerez Hiltonis banned from TikTok
UN Chief Antonio Guterres urges world's leaders to declare "climate emergency" to avoid catastrophic global warning on 5th anniversary of Paris Climate Accord
DutchmanMax Verstappenwins Formula 1 Drivers' C'ship for Red Bull with a controversial last lap win over Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonin the season-ending Abu Dhabi GP
Six-time Super Bowl winning coachBill Belichickis introduced as next head coach at the University of North Carolina
Utrecht, Overijssel, Buren, Leerdam and Ijsselstein adopt Gregorian calendar
Pennsylvania becomes 2nd state to ratify US constitution
Guglielmo Marconisends the first transatlantic radio signal, from Poldhu in Cornwall to Newfoundland, Canada
Nazi German army officerAdolf Eichmannis found guilty of war crimes in Israel
50‐year‐old Georgia GovernorJimmy Carterannounces his candidacy for the Democratic nomination for President
"Saturday Night Fever", film starringJohn Travolta, with music by the Bee Gees, premieres in NYC

Georges Jean Pfeiffer, French composer, born in Versailles, France (d. 1908)

Johan Petter Johansson, Swedish inventor and industrialist (modern adjustable spanner), born in Vårgårda, Sweden (d. 1943)
Edward R. Bradley, American businessman and horse owner (1st to own 4 Kentucky Derby winners), born in Johnstown, Pennsylvania (d. 1946)
Maurice Donnay, French playwright (Lovers), born in Paris (d. 1945)
Paul Elmer More, American critic and essayist, born in Saint Louis, Missouri (d. 1937)
Alfred Werner, Swiss chemist (Nobel 1913), born in Mulhouse, Haut-Rhin, Alsace, France (d. 1919)

Alvin Kraenzlein, American track and field athlete (4 Olympic gold 1900), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota (d. 1928)

Leonora Summers [Lillian Hill], American vaudeville and silent film actress [Ben-Hur: A Tale of the Christ; Hoboken To Hollywood), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1976)

Frederik van Heek, Dutch sociologist, born in Enschede, Netherlands (d. 1987)

Armand Boni, Flemish poet and writer, born in Antwerp, Belgium (d. 1991)

Eve Lister, British film and television actress (No Limit, Hyde Park), born in Brighton, Sussex, England (d. 1997)

Hal Dickinson, American singer (Modernaires), born in Buffalo, New York (d. 1970)
Stanley Bate, English composer, born in Plymouth, England (d. 1959)
Buzzie Bavasi, American MLB executive (Brooklyn & LA Dodgers), born in New York City (d. 2008)
Denis Begbie, South African cricketer (South African batsman in five Tests 1948-50), born in Middelburg, Transvaal, South Africa (d. 2009)
Edward Firth Henderson, British diplomat and arabist, born in Bristol (d. 1995)
Joe Williams [Joseph Goreed], American jazz singer ("Everyday I Have the Blues"), born in Cordele, Georgia (d. 1999)
Nilda Pinto, Curacao writer (Nanzi), born in Otrobanda (d. 1954)
Dan DeCarlo, American cartoonist (Archie Comics), born in New Rochelle, New York (d. 2001)
Olivia Barclay, British astrologer (revival of traditional forms of astrology in the late 20th century), born in Essex, England (d. 2001)
Dick James [Leon Vapnick], British dance band vocalist and Beatles' music publisher (Northern Songs), born in London, England (d. 1986)
Fred Kida, Japanese-American comics artist, born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2014)
Arthur R. Hawkins, American naval aviator and flying ace (WW II, downed 14 Japanese planes), born in Zavalla, Texas (d. 2004)
Bob Dorough, American jazz vocalist, pianist, composer, songwriter, arranger, and producer (Schoolhouse Rock!; Too Much Coffee Man), born in Cherry Hill, Arkansas (d. 2018)

Emahoy Tsegué-Maryam [Yewubdar Guèbrou], Ethiopian nun,  pianist, and composer ("The Homeless Wanderer"), born in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (d. 2023)
John Pulman, English snooker player (World Champion 1957-68; News of the World Snooker Tournament 1954, 57, 58), born in Teignmouth, England (d. 1998)
Ken Kavanagh, Australian motorcycle road racer (5 x GP wins; first Australian to win a Grand Prix race), born in Melbourne, Australia (d. 2019)

(Michael) "Dodo" Marmarosa, American big band and bebop jazz pianist, composer, and arranger (Artie Shaw), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 2002)
Ahmad Shamlou, Iranian poet (The Book of Alley), born in Tehran, Iran (d. 2000)
Anne V. Coates, British film editor (Lawrence of Arabia, The Elephant Man), born in Reigate, England (d. 2018)
Cora Lee Johnson, American social activist for rural poor, born in Georgia (d. 2021)
Dattu Phadkar, Indian cricketer (pace bowling all-rounder for India), born in Kolhapur, India (d. 1985)
